title: Harness vocabulary explainer
summary: Decision: use local-original definitions to explain Harness Hub concepts without copying external glossary text.

intent.primaryQuestion: How should effective-interact explain local harness vocabulary?
intent.decision: Use definition, adjacent concept contrast, avoid/use-instead wording, usage scenario, evidence, and acceptance boundary.
successCriteria: The artifact stays useful without copying external glossary text.

cards.label: Source boundary
cards.detail: Use the dictionary repo as a structure reference, not copied content.

cards.label: Local source
cards.detail: Harness Hub owns the actual definitions and examples.

cards.label: Output shape
cards.detail: Every concept needs the nearest adjacent term and misuse warning.

sections.title: BLUF
sections.content:
**Use vocabulary explainers to prevent wrong routing and false validation claims.**

- Keep the first screen focused on the term boundary.
- Use adjacent concepts to show what would change the next action.
- Treat source links as evidence, not text to copy.

sections.title: Required term shape
sections.summary: The same rows can be used for model/agent, skill/tool, or check/review distinctions.

rows.purpose: State the Harness Hub meaning in one local-original paragraph.
rows.failure: The reader imports a vendor definition that does not match this repo.

rows.purpose: Name the closest term that is easy to confuse.
rows.failure: The report treats a skill like a tool or a check like a review.

rows.purpose: Show wording that would change routing, safety, or validation meaning.
rows.failure: The artifact sounds precise while preserving the wrong boundary.

rows.purpose: Anchor the term in one repo-local maintenance or handoff situation.
rows.failure: The glossary becomes abstract documentation instead of decision support.

rows.purpose: Name the evidence or decision that proves the explanation worked.
rows.failure: The report ends with explanation but no actionable next step.

sections.title: Adjacent concept examples
sections.summary: Each tab models the contrast pattern without copying external entries.

tabs.label: skill vs tool
tabs.content: Definition: a skill is conditional context the agent reads; a tool is a callable action surface. Avoid saying a skill runs commands. Use instead: the skill instructs the agent when to call a tool.

tabs.label: check vs review
tabs.content: Definition: an automated check is deterministic pass/fail; automated review is judgement. Avoid calling an LLM reviewer a check unless the assertion is deterministic.

tabs.label: handoff vs compaction
tabs.content: Definition: a handoff artifact is explicit and inspectable; compaction is lossy session carryover. Avoid treating a compacted summary as the only proof of completed work.

sections.title: Harness Hub vocabulary anchors
sections.summary: These anchors keep local explainers aligned with repo policy.

rows.acceptance: No copied entry text or installable component.
Terms separate model/agent/harness, skill/tool, and check/review.

rows.role: Constrains vocabulary normalization to local or deeply adapted skills.
rows.acceptance: Imported skill bodies remain preserved by default.

rows.role: Turns concept boundaries into a reusable explainer pattern.
rows.acceptance: Research explainers include definition and adjacent-concept contrast.

sections.title: Next action
items: Use this explainer shape when a Harness Hub report depends on terms such as skill, tool, harness, check, review, handoff, or compaction.
Stay in Markdown when one compact table explains the distinction.
Escalate to HTML only when source anchors, tabs, or side-by-side concept comparisons lower decision cost.

claims.statement: Harness Hub vocabulary explainers must use local-original definitions rather than copied external glossary text.
The reusable explainer value is the adjacent-concept boundary, not a larger glossary.

evidence.label: dictionary-of-ai-coding upstream
knownLimits: No license file or package license field was present in the reviewed snapshot.

verification.label: source boundary
verification.detail: The fixture references upstream as source inspiration only and uses local-original definitions.

verification.label: explainer structure
verification.detail: The artifact includes definition, adjacent concept, avoid/use-instead, usage scenario, and acceptance boundary.

nextActions: Validate the generated HTML with validate-interaction.mjs.
Use docs/harness-vocabulary.md as the local term source for future Harness Hub explainers.
